free_text: Neutron capture between 5 keV and 3 MeV
Preliminary results.
In calculating the fission cross
section of the  uranium monitor (93.27% 235U, 1.03%
used were those of P.H. White J.Nuc.En.19, 325(1965);
values for the other isotopes were taken from
D.J. Hughes et al. (1960).
U
neutron flux monitor foil cs.
Monitor foils cs.
Monoenergetic neutrons between 150 keV and 1
MeV were produced by the 7Li(p,n)7Be reaction, and
between 1 and 3 MeV by the 3H(p,n) 3He reaction.
Neutron intensity was measured with a fission
counter, a parallel-plate ionization chamber, whose
fissile species was enriched uranium in a 1-cm
diameter deposit of mass 250 microg, on a thin
platinum foil. During irradiation, the targets lay
flat against the monitor foil, both subtending the
same polar angle in the neutron beam. The neutron
flux was monitored relatively with a long counter at
120 degrees at a distance of 2 m from the source. The
relative cross sections so obtained were normalized
to those determined absolutely at the higher energies
in this work, by performing irradiations at 120
degrees with neutron energy high enough to overlap
those done in the forward direction. Following
irradiation, the disk-shaped targets were analysed
for their activation products either by counting
their beta-rays with an end-window proportional
counter, or by taking their gamma-ray spectra with a
4x4 in. NaI crystal and multichannel analyser.
The errors attached to the cross sections
are standard deviations.
Translated from SCISRS
converted to reaction formalism
BP: Updated references, added doi, decay
data, error analysis, detectors, sample and flux
monitor corrections. Updated to new date formats,
lower case. Corrections according last EXFOR rules
and Dict.
